nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

nameMeanings.name: Silas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of New Testament origin: Greek name, a contracted form of Silouanus (Latin Silvanus, a derivative of silva ‘wood’). This name was borne by a companion of St Paul, who is also mentioned in the Bible in the full form of his name. The Eastern Church recognizes two separate saints, Silas and Silvanus, but honours both on the same day ( 20 July ).

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: South Carolina becomes the 8th state in the Union
historicEvents.description: On May 23, 1788, South Carolina ratifies the Constitution of the United States making it the 8th State of the Union.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Walton War
historicEvents.description: The Walton War was a border dispute between Georgia and North Carolina, when Georgia established Walton County. The main piece of land being fought over was called the Orphan Strip and was located between North Carolina and Georgia. It started in 1804 and became part of the War of 1812 and lasted until 1818.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The War Between Georgia and North Carolina

historicEvents.title: Fort Sumter Constructed
historicEvents.description: In 1829 Fort Sumter is constructed in the Charleston Harbor, South Carolina. Fort Sumter is most known for being the site of the first shots of the Civil War. It is barely ready when the American Civil War starts.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: First State Fair
historicEvents.description: The first state fair in North Carolina was held in Raleigh and was put on by the North Carolina State Agricultural Society in 1853. The fair has been continuous except for during the American Civil War and Reconstruction and WWII.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

en.lifeSummary: When Silas Massey was born on 6 March 1776, in Johnston, North Carolina, United States, his father, Noel Posey Massey, was 20 and his mother, Lucinda Freeman, was 20. He married Nancy Burris in 1802. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 9 daughters. He died on 11 September 1876, in Anderson, South Carolina, United States, at the age of 100, and was buried in Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, South Carolina, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Silas Massey est né le 6 mars 1776, à Johnston, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, son père, Noel Posey Massey, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lucinda Freeman, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Nancy Burris en 1802. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé le 11 septembre 1876, à Anderson, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, à l’âge de 100 ans, et a été enterré à Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Silas Massey è nato il il 6 marzo 1776 nel Johnston, Carolina del Nord, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Noel Posey Massey, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Lucinda Freeman, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Nancy Burris nel 1802. Erano genitori di almeno 11 figli e 9 figlie. Morì il 11 settembre 1876, nel Anderson, Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 100 anni, e fu sepolto a Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Silas Masseyが1776年3月6日にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ジョンストンで生まれた時，父Noel Posey Masseyは20歳，母Lucinda Freemanは20歳であった。1802年にNancy Burrisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1876年9月11日にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州アンダーソンで100歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州アンダーソンモウンティイン クレエク チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, South C: 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨 모우테인 크리크 c후르치
ko.Honea Path, Anderson, South Carolina, Un: 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨 호니아 패스
ko.lifeSummary: Silas Massey(이)가 1776년 3월 6일 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 존스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Noel Posey Massey(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucinda Freeman(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1802년 Nancy Burris(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1876년 9월 11일 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨에서 100세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨 모우테인 크리크 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Silas Massey nasceu em 6 de março de 1776, em Johnston, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Noel Posey Massey, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Lucinda Freeman, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Nancy Burris em 1802. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de setembro de 1876, em Anderson, Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América, com 100 anos, e foi sepultado em Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Silas Massey родился 6 марта 1776 г. в местности джонстон, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Noel Posey Massey было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucinda Freeman, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Nancy Burris в 1802 г. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 9 дочерей. Он умер 11 сентября 1876 г. в местности андирсон, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 100 лет и был похоронен в местности маунтеин крик чурч, андирсон, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Silas Massey nació el 6 de marzo de 1776, en Johnston, Carolina del Norte, Estados Unidos, su papá, Noel Posey Massey, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Lucinda Freeman, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Nancy Burris en 1802. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 11 de septiembre de 1876, en Anderson, Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 100 años, y fue sepultado en Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos.
